name: docx (Word Document Generator for JavaScript)

performance.notes: Pure JavaScript OOXML generation; proportional to document size

agent_readiness.notes: DOCX (.docx) generation for Node.js and browser. import { Document, Paragraph, TextRun, HeadingLevel, AlignmentType, Table, TableRow, TableCell, Packer } from 'docx'. const doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [new Paragraph({ text: 'Hello', heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1 }), new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun({ text: 'Bold', bold: true }), new TextRun(' normal')] })] }] }). const buffer = await Packer.toBuffer(doc). const blob = await Packer.toBlob(doc). Table: new Table({ rows: [new TableRow({ children: [new TableCell({ children: [new Paragraph('A')] })] })] }). Images: new ImageRun({ data: fs.readFileSync('./img.png'), transformation: { width: 200, height: 100 } }). Styles, lists, page breaks, headers/footers.

gotchas: Write-only — cannot read/modify existing .docx files (use mammoth.js for reading)
Verbose API — even simple paragraphs require nested Document/Section/Paragraph structure
Complex table formatting requires many options — refer to docs carefully
Images require dimensions explicitly — no auto-sizing
Custom styles require style ID registration at Document level
best_when: When Microsoft Word compatibility is required (vs PDF-only)
Documents with rich formatting: tables, images, headers/footers, lists
avoid_when: Modifying existing .docx (use LibreOffice UNO or python-docx)
Simple text documents where PDF is acceptable
summary: docx generates Microsoft Word (.docx) files. Document({sections: [{children: [new Paragraph(...)]}]}). Packer.toBuffer() / Packer.toBlob(). Tables, images, headings, lists, headers/footers. MIT by Dolan Miu. Type-annotated. Write-only. Best for server-side Word document generation. Verbose nested API. Images need explicit dimensions. Use mammoth.js to read DOCX. Supports rich formatting for professional documents.
